sound_type: rapid bark / honk -- classic 'arf arf arf' sea lion bark. Rapid, rhythmic, insistent. Fills harbors and docks. E=6
ecosystem: Docks, rocks, marinas, harbors, offshore islands. Expanding range into PNW waters. Urban waterfront presence.
season: Present in PNW year-round (males especially). Breeding in California/Mexico, June-July. Non-breeding northward migration.

coordinates.description: California Sea Lion on PNW docks and harbors. Expanding range. Visible in urban waterfront environments.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Otariidae. The 'circus seal' -- most familiar pinniped. Steller/California comparison pair (182-183). Exceptionally intelligent and trainable. MMPA protected.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Otariidae: Steller Sea Lion (182), California Sea Lion (183) -- 2 of 3.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Zalophus from Greek 'za' (intensive) + 'lophos' (crest) -- prominent crest. californianus for California, primary breeding range.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Zalophus -- 3 species (1 extinct). Z. californianus is the common California Sea Lion.
taxonomy_note.comparison_pair: Steller (182) vs California (183): largest vs most familiar, offshore vs dock, deep roar vs rapid bark, endangered vs thriving.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 200-4000 Hz. Higher-pitched than Steller Sea Lion.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual bark: 0.2-0.5 seconds. Rapid repetition: 2-4 per second. Continuous for minutes.
song_metrics.note_structure: Rapid, rhythmic, honking bark. 'Arf arf arf.' Classic sea lion sound.
song_metrics.note_types: Bark (primary), mother-pup recognition call, aggressive growl, underwater bark.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Rapid repetition in series. 2-4 barks per second. Colony: overlapping continuous barking.
song_metrics.timbre: Honking, nasal, rhythmic. Higher and faster than Steller roar. Insistent.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=6. Loud. Dock acoustics amplify. Colony creates wall of sound. Speed + persistence = E=6.
song_metrics.tempo: Bark rate: 2-4 per second. Rapid. Rhythmic. Relentless.
song_metrics.context: Docks, haul-outs, marinas. Social, territorial, alarm, continuous.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Individual vocal signatures for mother-pup recognition.
song_metrics.key_property: RAPID BARK. 'Circus seal.' Most recognizable pinniped. Exceptionally intelligent. E=6. v1.49.324.

pelage.overall: Dark brown (wet) to tan/golden brown (dry). Males darker. Sleek coat, shorter than fur seal.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: External ear flaps. Dog-like face. Sagittal crest on males. Can walk on all four flippers. Sleek body.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Males develop fuller crest with maturity.
pelage.sexual_dimorphism: Males: 2.4m, 390 kg. Females: 1.8m, 110 kg. ~3.5:1 weight ratio.

physical_description.overall: Males: length 2.4m, weight up to 390 kg. Females: 1.8m, up to 110 kg. Sleek body. External ear flaps. Sagittal crest (males).
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: External ear flaps. Dog-like face. Dark brown to tan. Walks on flippers. Rapid barking.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-large sea lion on docks or rocks. Dark brown to tan. External ear flaps visible. Dog-like face. Listen for rapid 'arf arf arf' bark.

nodes.title: The Performer -- Everyone Knows This One
nodes.description: The 'circus seal.' Trained by US Navy. Aquarium star. Most recognizable pinniped. Exceptional intelligence and trainability. The one people picture when they hear 'sea lion.'

nodes.title: Rapid Bark -- Speed and Persistence
nodes.description: 2-4 barks per second. 200-4000 Hz. E=6 through rapid repetition rather than depth. Dock acoustics amplify. Classic 'arf arf arf.'

nodes.title: Steller/California Comparison (182-183)
nodes.description: Same family, same energy, different animals. Steller: giant, offshore, endangered. California: performer, docks, thriving. Full Otariidae range documented.

cross_references.relationship: COMPARISON PAIR! Largest vs most familiar. Deep roar vs rapid bark. Offshore vs docks. Endangered vs thriving. Same family, same energy.
OTARIIDAE TRIO! Sea lion pair (182-183) + fur seal (184). Three eared seals. Fur seal completes the family sequence.
PINNIPED CONTRAST! Phocidae vs Otariidae. True seal vs eared seal. No external ears vs external ears. Different families.
THE PERFORMER! Both command attention. Raz Simone on Capitol Hill, Cold Recordings. Sea lion on the dock. Presence and performance.
